root_text.an5.169:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā sāriputtena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an5.169:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.169:2.1: “Kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, bhikkhu khippanisanti ca hoti, kusalesu dhammesu suggahitaggāhī ca, bahuñca gaṇhāti, gahitañcassa nappamussatī”ti?
root_text.an5.169:2.4: “Tenahāvuso sāriputta, suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an5.169:2.5: “Evamāvuso”ti kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmato ānandassa paccassosi.
root_text.an5.169:3.1: “Idhāvuso sāriputta, bhikkhu atthakusalo ca hoti, dhammakusalo ca, byañjanakusalo ca, niruttikusalo ca, pubbāparakusalo ca.
root_text.an5.169:3.2: Ettāvatā kho, āvuso sāriputta, bhikkhu khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu, suggahitaggāhī ca, bahuñca gaṇhāti, gahitañcassa nappamussatī”ti.
root_text.an5.169:3.5: Imehi ca mayaṁ pañcahi dhammehi samannāgataṁ āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ dhārema:
root_text.an5.169:3.6: ‘āyasmā ānando atthakusalo dhammakusalo byañjanakusalo niruttikusalo pubbāparakusalo’”ti.

translation_text.an5.169:1.1: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to Venerable Sāriputta, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an5.169:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to him:
translation_text.an5.169:2.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, how are we to define a mendicant who is quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings, who learns well, learns much, and does not forget what they’ve learned?”
translation_text.an5.169:2.3: Why don’t you clarify this yourself?”
translation_text.an5.169:2.4: “Well then, Reverend Sāriputta, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an5.169:3.1: “It’s when a mendicant is skilled in the meaning, skilled in the teaching, skilled in definition, skilled in phrasing, and skilled in sequence.
translation_text.an5.169:3.2: That is how to define a mendicant who is quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings, who learns well, learns much, and does not forget what they’ve learned.”
translation_text.an5.169:3.4: How well this was said by Venerable Ānanda!
translation_text.an5.169:3.5: And we will remember Venerable Ānanda as someone who possesses these five qualities:
translation_text.an5.169:3.6: ‘Reverend Ānanda is skilled in the meaning, skilled in the teaching, skilled in definition, skilled in phrasing, and skilled in sequence.’”
